name: multiple-issues-one-clear
description: Two findings: (1) customer-support-bot 30% error rate from rate limit exceeded, 45 traces. (2) email-classifier latency up 20% but within range. Rate limit is primary.

item.title: Multiple anomalies across agents
item.description: Automated alert: error rate threshold exceeded on customer-support-bot and latency threshold exceeded on email-classifier.
item.note:
Investigated both alerts simultaneously. Two separate findings:

**Finding 1 — customer-support-bot rate limiting (CRITICAL)**
- Error rate: 30% in the last 2 hours (45 of 150 traces)
- All errors: "rate_limit_exceeded" (HTTP 429) from OpenAI
- Errors started at 12:00 UTC, ongoing as of 14:05 UTC
- The rate limit is on the gpt-4-turbo tier — we're hitting 500 RPM limit
- Trace volume is elevated: 75/hour vs normal ~25/hour
- Affected traces return a 500 to the end user with "Service temporarily unavailable"
- No retry logic in place for rate limit errors
- trace-ids: multi-3301, multi-3315, multi-3328, multi-3342 (sampled)

**Finding 2 — email-classifier latency increase (LOW)**
- p95 latency: 620ms (vs 500ms baseline) — a 24% increase
- p50 latency: 410ms (vs 380ms baseline) — an 8% increase
- 52 traces in the last 2 hours, 0 errors
- The increase is within acceptable operational range (SLO is 1000ms)
- Likely caused by OpenAI API latency variance — no code changes
- No user impact

The rate limit issue on customer-support-bot is the clear priority. It's actively causing user-facing failures. The email-classifier latency is a non-issue.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
